evidence.scientific_clarity: Scatter + OLS line + R² annotation + OursModel highlight all present; title, axis labels, and in-figure stats box make the scaling-law claim immediately legible.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set in script; PDF embeds Type 3 DejaVu Sans (bitmapped, non-selectable); SVG text rendered as paths — text is not editable in Illustrator/Inkscape.
evidence.palette: Blue (#4878CF) scatter, red (#E84040) OLS line, orange (#F5A623) highlight — no green/red directional clash; red-orange pair may be confused under deuteranopia, but overall reasonably CB-safe.
evidence.layout_density: 6×4.5 in figure, tight_layout used, annotation box top-right, legend top-left, OursModel arrow offset avoids overlap; no obvious whitespace waste or label collisions.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption values (slope, intercept, R²) match script output; OursModel coordinates stated precisely; legend present; minor issue: red used for fit line could be flagged as directional color by a pedantic reviewer.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is real vector (21 KB, PDF 1.4) but fonts are Type 3 (pdffonts confirms GCWXDV+DejaVuSans-Oblique and BMQQDV+DejaVuSans as Type 3) — text is not selectable/searchable.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; figure.svg also produced; data.json absent from work directory (hardcoded absolute path in script).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the plot type, axis scales, sample size (80 models), gives exact slope/intercept/R² values, interprets R²=0.877 as '~88% variance explained', and identifies OursModel coordinates — strong numerical specificity.

overall_strengths: All brief requirements met: scatter, OLS fit, R² annotation, and OursModel highlight are correctly implemented and the caption precisely echoes the in-figure statistics.
Layout is clean and dense — tight_layout, sensible annotation placement, grid at low alpha, and a well-sized figure with no wasted whitespace.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 / svg.fonttype=0: PDF fonts are Type 3 (bitmapped DejaVu Sans), making text non-selectable and non-editable in vector editors — a hard requirement for journal submission.
Default DejaVu Sans font used throughout; no explicit font configuration in the script, and the red-orange color pair for OLS line vs. OursModel point has mild deuteranopia risk.
